mnemonics: Don't STOP the Force, Interrupt it (use interrupt() instead of stop())
keyPoints: Use a volatile boolean flag to signal a thread to stop itself.
The interrupt() method is the preferred and cooperative way to request a thread to stop.
A thread must periodically check its interrupt status or handle InterruptedException to respond to a stop request.
commonMistakes: Using the deprecated Thread.stop() method, which can leave shared data in an inconsistent state.
Not handling InterruptedException properly, often by just catching and ignoring it, which prevents the thread from stopping.
Using a non-volatile variable as a stop flag, which can lead to visibility issues between threads.
